refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR includes multiple obligations: analysis of terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To ensure atomicity and clarity, these should be separated into distinct sub-FRs. This refined FR focuses solely on terrain representation, which is a fundamental aspect of worksite area analysis.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate and provide accurate terrain data of the worksite area, independent of obstacle or work-zone data.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including obstacle representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including work-zone representation.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing the physical features of the worksite
Create a semantic map annotating key site features relevant to planning and safety
Develop a layered worksite model combining terrain and operationally relevant features
Produce a geospatial database capturing terrain and site feature attributes separately
Implement a modular mapping framework separating terrain representation from feature annotation
selected_dp.selected_dp: Develop a layered worksite model combining terrain and operationally relevant features

potential_sources: Integration of terrain and site features in a single model may create dependencies if not layered properly
coupling_check.notes: By structuring the worksite model in layers (terrain layer plus feature annotation layers), the design supports independence and modular updates, reducing coupling between terrain representation and site feature data.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate terrain and site feature data for model generation
Risk that layering is not properly implemented, leading to tightly coupled data structures
Assumes planning and execution functions can consume layered model format
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify that the worksite model should be structured in modular layers to support independent updates and integration with planning/execution functions.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the overall system responsible for environmental modeling, data integration, and operational feature synthesis at the worksite level.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives sensor and vehicle state data to update the worksite model.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives operational commands that may influence model updates or feature relevance.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports data exchange with external systems and remote components for model synchronization.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as the layered worksite model is central to the NGHE Solution; minimal diffusion as most data and processing are internal.
Vehicle module uses the model but does not create or maintain it; would cause significant diffusion if responsible for model development.
Teleoperation relies on the model for remote control but is not responsible for its development; high diffusion if contained here.
Safety Services may use model data for alerts but is not involved in model creation; very poor containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for containing the DP of developing a layered worksite model, as it centralizes environmental data processing and operational feature integration, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential complexity within NGHE Solution may lead to internal coupling if not well modularized.
Interfaces with Vehicle and Teleoperation must be well defined to avoid unintended diffusion of model logic.
Changes in operational features may require frequent updates to the model, risking diffusion if responsibilities are not clearly maintained.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP of developing a layered worksite model combining terrain and operationally relevant features directly addresses the FR of providing worksite area analysis including terrain representation. The DP logically decomposes the FR into a solution concept that meets the acceptance criteria of generating a worksite model/map available to planning and execution functions.

diffusion_risks: Interfaces with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules could lead to diffusion if model update responsibilities are not strictly confined to the NGHE Solution module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating strong cohesion and appropriate ownership of the DP. Other modules have low containment and high diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the IM boundary. However, care must be taken to maintain clear interface definitions to prevent diffusion of model logic across modules.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the NGHE Solution module is responsible for generating the worksite model/map, which can be validated against acceptance criteria such as availability to planning/execution functions and inclusion of required site features.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the FR to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and well justified by the module containment scores and scope description, ensuring ownership and responsibility for the worksite model.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly define and document interfaces between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Teleoperation modules to prevent diffusion of model update logic.
Consider internal modularization within NGHE Solution to manage complexity and maintain low internal coupling.
Establish clear update protocols for operational feature changes to avoid responsibility overlap and diffusion risks.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification and verification feasibility. The NGHE Solution module is well defined as the IM with high containment and low diffusion, and the DP logically addresses the FR. Although there are diffusion risks related to interfaces with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, these are manageable through interface definition and update protocols without requiring further decomposition of the FR at this stage. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining clear module boundaries and verification is prioritized over further decomposition, which could increase complexity and risk.

revise.why: To explicitly define and document interfaces between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Teleoperation modules to prevent diffusion of model update logic, and to consider internal modularization within NGHE Solution to manage complexity and maintain low internal coupling.

assumptions: The current DP sufficiently captures the functional requirement for worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
The NGHE Solution module can be internally modularized without changing the top-level FR decomposition.
Interface definitions and update protocols can effectively mitigate diffusion risks.
open_risks: Potential diffusion of model update responsibilities across Vehicle and Teleoperation modules if interfaces are not strictly managed.
Complexity within NGHE Solution module may increase if internal modularization is not carefully designed.
evidence_needed: Verification results confirming the worksite model/map meets acceptance criteria and is available to planning and execution functions.
Documentation of interface definitions and update protocols between NGHE Solution and other modules.
Containment and diffusion metrics after internal modularization of NGHE Solution.
